repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
This PR supports type inference of builtin type container constructors. Hence, subscripted type is correctly propagated:

```cython
l: list[int] = [1,2,3]
s = set(l)   # s variable is of type set[int]
```
The PR consists of two parts:
1. implementation and tests
2. migration type inference tests to separate file based on https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/7729#issuecomment-4583410709

This PR needs to be adjusted when #7704 is merged

comment_data.body: I would like to carve out this code somehow in separate function to increase readability...
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6226,7 +6226,15 @@ def infer_type(self, env):
                         return PyrexTypes.c_double_type
                     elif func_name == 'bool':
                         return PyrexTypes.c_bint_type
-                    elif func_name in Builtin.types_that_construct_their_instance:
+                    elif func_name in [name for name, typ in Builtin.builtin_types.items() if typ.supports_container_type]:

comment_data.body:
Isn't this just
```suggestion
                    elif result_type.supports_container_type and len(self.args) == 1:
```
I haven't tried it but I don't think we need the `hasattr()` check here. That might be needed for the C function case a bit up, but here we're dealing with a call to a builtin type. That should always have `self.args`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6226,6 +6226,19 @@ def infer_type(self, env):
                         return PyrexTypes.c_double_type
                     elif func_name == 'bool':
                         return PyrexTypes.c_bint_type
+                    elif (
+                        hasattr(self, 'args') and self.args and len(self.args) == 1 and
+                        func_name in [name for name, typ in Builtin.builtin_types.items() if typ.supports_container_type]
+                    ):

comment_data.body: It would be better to use a decorator (`@min_plain_pyversion(3,11)`) for this kind of skipping. Once we drop support for Py<3.10 or fix/work around the issue, it'll be easier to remove the decorator line than to realign the whole function, thus losing its VCS line history.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,352 @@
+# cython: language_level=3
+# mode: run
+
+import sys
+import cython
+
+from typing import Dict, List
+try:
+    import typing
+    from typing import Set as _SET_
+except ImportError:
+    pass  # this should allow Cython to interpret the directives even when the module doesn't exist
+
+
+
+def test_subscripted_types():
+    """
+    >>> test_subscripted_types()
+    dict[int,float] object
+    dict[int,float] object
+    list[int] object
+    list[int] object
+    list object
+    set[Python object] object
+    """
+    a1: typing.Dict[cython.int, cython.float] = {}
+    a2: dict[cython.int, cython.float] = {}
+    b1: List[cython.int] = []
+    b2: list[cython.int] = []
+    b3: List = []  # doesn't need to be subscripted
+    c: _SET_[object] = set()
+
+    print(cython.typeof(a1) + ("[int,float] object" if not cython.compiled else ""))
+    print(cython.typeof(a2) + ("[int,float] object" if not cython.compiled else ""))
+    print(cython.typeof(b1) + ("[int] object" if not cython.compiled else ""))
+    print(cython.typeof(b2) + ("[int] object" if not cython.compiled else ""))
+    print(cython.typeof(b3) + (" object" if not cython.compiled else ""))
+    print(cython.typeof(c) + ("[Python object] object" if not cython.compiled else ""))
+
+if sys.version_info >= (3, 11) or cython.compiled:
+    # This part of the test is failing in Python 3.9 and 3.10 with the following exception in Shadow.py:
+    # isinstance() argument 2 cannot be a parameterized generic
+
+    def test_casting_subscripted_types():

comment_data.body:
From a quick look, I couldn't find a test for something like this:
```python
items: list[tuple[int, str]] = [(1, 'xaz')]
assert cython.typeof(dict(items)) == "dict[int, str]"
```
Is that covered here?

```suggestion
                                    subscripted_types = st.subscripted_types
```
(Although, if you need a tuple, then it would be `subscripted_types = tuple(st.subscripted_types)`).

@@ -6245,6 +6245,27 @@ def infer_type(self, env):
                         return PyrexTypes.c_double_type

+                    elif result_type.supports_container_type and len(self.args) == 1:
+                        func_arg_type = self.args[0].infer_type(env)
+                        if func_arg_type.supports_container_type:
+                            if func_name in ['dict', 'frozendict']:

comment_data.body:
Dicts are special e.g. `dict(d: dict[str, int])` -> `dict[str, int]`

`.infer_iterator_type()` will for `d` in example above is just `str` which is wrong. Not to mention fun when dict is constructed from list of tuples or list of lists...

comment_data.body:
Well it is questionable how to proceed. Python supports following:

```python
l: list[list[int]] = [[1,2], [3, 4]]
d = dict(list)
```
In the end d will contain: `{1: 2, 3: 4}`

But we can just make `d` as plain `dict` it is possible

comment_data.body:
> Python supports following:
> ```python
> l: list[list[int]] = [[1,2], [3, 4]]
> d = dict(list)
> ```

I don't think that is an issue. Even if we do not know the length of the non-tuple items at compile time, if it's a homogeneous sequence, then either the key-value pairs both have that type, or the dict creation will fail at runtime if it doesn't find key-value pairs. So we can always infer the key-value types from a homogeneous sequence.

+                        func_arg_type = self.args[0].infer_type(env)
+                        if func_arg_type.supports_container_type:
+                            if func_name in ['dict', 'frozendict']:
+                                # We infer subscripted type of new dictionary only if type of the argument
+                                # is CONTAINER[CONTAINER[...]]
+                                st = func_arg_type.subscripted_types[0] if len(func_arg_type.subscripted_types) == 1 else None
+                                if func_arg_type.is_pyanydict_type:

+                                elif st and st.is_pytuple_type and len(st.subscripted_types) == 2:

+                                    # Hence, we just take first type and hope for the best. In any case,
+                                    # the runtime will error out when there are more than two elements.
+                                    subscripted_types = (st.subscripted_types[0], st.subscripted_types[0])

comment_data.body:
It is a slighly mess:

```python
 frozendict()
```

yields `posargs` as a `tuple` => `len(posargs)` ✅ 

On the other hand:

```python
frozendict(parrot=u"resting", answer=42)
```

yields `posargs` as `TupleNode` => `len(posargs)` raises an exception ❌  (one must do additional `posargs.args`)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6243,15 +6243,74 @@ def infer_type(self, env):
                     func_name = function.entry.name
                     if func_name == 'float':
                         return PyrexTypes.c_double_type
-                    elif func_name == 'bool':
+                    if func_name == 'bool':
                         return PyrexTypes.c_bint_type
-                    elif func_name in Builtin.types_that_construct_their_instance:
+                    # Handle both SimpleCallNode (.args) and GeneralCallNode (.positional_args / .keyword_args).
+                    posargs = getattr(self, 'args', getattr(self, 'positional_args', ()))
+                    if result_type.supports_container_type and len(posargs) == 1 and not getattr(self, 'keyword_args', None):

comment_data.body:
I just noticed that we're excluding `GeneralCallNode` already by testing for kwargs, so let's exclude it earlier.
```suggestion
                    if result_type.supports_container_type and not isinstance(self, GeneralCallNode) and len(self.args) == 1:
                        arg = self.args[0]
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -6243,15 +6243,75 @@ def infer_type(self, env):
                     func_name = function.entry.name
                     if func_name == 'float':
                         return PyrexTypes.c_double_type
-                    elif func_name == 'bool':
+                    if func_name == 'bool':
                         return PyrexTypes.c_bint_type
-                    elif func_name in Builtin.types_that_construct_their_instance:
+                    # Handle both SimpleCallNode (.args) and GeneralCallNode (.positional_args / .keyword_args).
+                    posargs = getattr(self, 'args', getattr(self, 'positional_args', ()))
+                    posargs = posargs.args if hasattr(posargs, 'args') else posargs
+                    if result_type.supports_container_type and len(posargs) == 1 and not getattr(self, 'keyword_args', None):
+                        arg = posargs[0]

repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

milestone.description:
Main features:
- Pattern matching
- Generics / subscripted container types
- `@cython.collection_type()`
- `restrict`
- more vectorcalls
- more thread safety
- smaller modules, more shared code
- remove Py3.8 support, keep Py3.9+

title: Allow type constructors `list()`, `set()`, ... to infer subscripted type
